title: Innovation-Driven Culture with Exceptional Support and Benefits
body: Innovation-Driven Culture with Exceptional Support and Benefits
pros: - Supportive Environment: The management team and colleagues are highly professional, smart, and consistently supportive. - Modern Tech & AI Integration: The company invests heavily in AI, providing developers with cutting-edge AI code assistants and tools to streamline workflows. - Excellent Onboarding: New developers benefit from a comprehensive, week-long onboarding class that effectively sets them up for success. Outstanding Benefits: Intuit offers top-tier insurance and a broad range of employee benefits. - NYC Office Amenities: The New York office features a modern design with incredible views (especially from the 8th floor), reservable workspaces, and a fully stocked kitchen with snacks and coffee. - Community Building: Weekly company-wide lunches on Thursdays are a great perk for team bonding.
cons: - Pace of Learning: Because the tech stack is so modern and AI-driven, the initial learning curve can be steep for those not used to fast-paced innovation. - Office Popularity: Since the NYC office is so nice, popular meeting rooms and desks can fill up quickly, requiring advance reservations.

title: Great Company Overall
pros: Strong benefits package and generally professional culture. Most colleagues are collaborative and respectful, and day to day interactions across teams tend to be positive. The company has talented people and a solid foundation that supports innovation and growth.
cons: Work life balance can become more challenging as responsibilities increase. Expectations and workload may expand faster than available time or resources, which can lead to sustained pressure during busy periods. Although time off is encouraged in principle, workload coverage structures may not always support true disconnection, and employees may return to a significant backlog. Professional development tends to rely heavily on individual initiative. While there is strong investment in product and business growth, some employees may feel there are fewer structured pathways for career development or skill building support. Goal setting can occasionally feel ambitious relative to available bandwidth, creating challenges in prioritization and execution.

title: Great Company, Nice Employees, Embrace Change
body: Great Company, Nice Employees, Embrace Change
pros: 1) I love the mission of powering the prosperity of businesses and seeing that in the work I do. 2) Intuit is very focused on employee experience and they do very well at it- best benefits I have ever had, campus is really nice in San Diego with events, gym, free fresh fruit all over campus, break rooms with free snacks, great food options, and more. 3) The people are nice
cons: 1) You do not start with any vacation time which is really tough. Positive is they have free recharge days around certain holidays so that helps a little. 2) Everyone is moving in the name of velocity, enabling scale, etc but if there was a little more time spent on strategy and thoughtfulness there wouldn't be as much constant change/chaos. Positive is if something isn't working they pivot. I see most times though leaders/drivers are working many things and make a quick decision without having an hour to really think it through and then they hastily make another decision in a different direction later. They say they move fast, but I see more of a lack of focus on better decision making.

title: A Supportive Place to Grow and Make an Impact
body: A Supportive Place to Grow and Make an Impact
pros: Supportive & Collaborative Culture Genuinely supportive team environment where colleagues are kind, approachable, and invested in each other's success. Collaboration feels natural, not forced. Meaningful, Mission-Driven Work There's real purpose behind the work â building products that make a tangible difference for small businesses and their customers. It's motivating to see the impact firsthand. Healthy Work-Life Balance Management respects boundaries and understands that sustainable performance comes from balance. Personal time is valued, not just tolerated. Culture of Innovation Plenty of room to experiment, iterate, and bring new ideas to the table. Curiosity and initiative are encouraged, not just accepted.
cons: Steep Initial Learning Curve Onboarding can feel overwhelming given the company's scale and the breadth of tools and systems to get up to speed on. It gets easier, but expect a ramp-up period.

title: Intense work culture but good balance
body: Intense work culture but good balance
pros: I joined the Mailchimp organization a few months ago, and the best part of both Intuit and our specific org is the leadership. They are genuinely presentâworking in the trenches with everyone, celebrating both small and big wins, and diving into low-level product and technical details. They hold frequent all-team meetings and discussions where they don't sugarcoat the organization's challenges; instead, they share very actionable plans. I havenât seen more engaged VP-level leadership anywhere else. All the other pros are well-known: the benefits are amazing, and the employee engagement activities are some of the best I have seen in the industry. Intuit overall company is very tech-focused and moves quickly when it comes to adopting new trends like AI.
cons: Synergies between orgs is very little and is showing in operational differences which sometimes results in conflicts.

title: Chaotic but as represented
pros: Wonderful team engagement ensuring my onboarding was as successful as possible. At no point did I feel left out or unattended. The documentation was helpful, although voluminous to the point of daunting. Intuit has a solid onboarding curriculum and LCPO has all the tools necessary to provide meaningful contributions on Day 1. The assigned "buddies" have been instrumental in my comfort and ability to contribute. They have (and continue to) fostered meaningful engagement and provided a wealth of institutional knowledge. Simply the best.
cons: Some choppiness during onboarding due to managerial shifts. But, I was always kept in the loop and never felt misdirected.

title: Love this company and my org!
body: Love this company and my org!
cons: Management challenges: At times, Iâve noticed inconsistencies in management qualityâsome managers struggle, while others are put in challenging situations that impact their ability to lead effectively. Frequent organizational changes: There can be a lot of change at the middle and upper management levels, which sometimes creates uncertainty. Workload during peak times: During peak periods, the workload can significantly increase, leading to long hours and potential burnoutâespecially around tax season or major deliverables.

title: The scales are not tipped in favor of the employee or even... in this EE/ER relationship.
body: The scales are not tipped in favor of the employee or even... in this EE/ER relationship.
pros: Remote work. Benefits. Resources and support.
cons: Inconsistent practices across the department. Metrics are easily manipulated by managers at their convenience. Discriminatory practices related to disability remain unaddressed. HR and management are misaligned on key business policies. Managers create processes that conflict with company policy, and lack of oversight allows these issues to continue. Monthly metric pulls are unreliable and not stored properly, leading to errors and unexplained fluctuations. Management demands on-time results, yet makes no improvements in areas repeatedly raised by employees.

title: Broken culture, lots of politics and toxic leadership
body: Broken culture, lots of politics and toxic leadership
pros: * Great equity and benefits. * Organization is still building itself so there is a lot of green-shoots work and a very low technical bar. * Incredibly easy interview process and hiring screen. * The company itself is stable and not going to dramatically change.
cons: * Data Science is focused more on name and degree recognition and appearing elite from the outside. * Most Managers have limited technical experience and cannot do the actual work of their ICs. * Stack-ranking leads to a very toxic work environment where people are looking to bring down others to boost their own team. * Any type of promotion past the Senior to Staff level is extremely difficult. Room for growth is limited due to lack of quality Principal, Senior Staff, or technical Managers. * Generally bad morale and company culture since the first major round of layoffs. RTO keeps creeping towards more and more days making the idea of hybrid work limited. * Work/life balance is atrocious and only getting worse. Everyone lies about how much they work so they sound like they are more efficient than they really are.

body: Toxic Work Culture leading to ZERO innovation
pros: Compensation and benefits. Employee perks
cons: Toxic leadership. Siloed decisions top-down with zero input or consultation even with SMEs and key contributors. Continous shift in priorities. 90% of the projects end up not getting implemented. HIGHLY political. Striving to become FAANG while forgetting the grounds and principles that the company once stood for.

title: Great culture, strong focus on tech, people and innovation
body: Great culture, strong focus on tech, people and innovation
pros: Intuit has a supportive and inclusive culture where people are encouraged to learn, grow, and make an impact. The company invests in innovation and provides opportunities to work on challenging, customer-focused problems. Leadership is approachable, and thereâs a strong emphasis on collaboration and respect. Like any large company, cross-team dependencies can slow things down at times, but overall itâs a great place to build a meaningful career.
cons: As with any large company, navigating cross-team dependencies can be challenging and requires patience. Growth can sometimes take time depending on business priorities and scope of work.

title: A company that invests in your growth and well-being
body: A company that invests in your growth and well-being
pros: 1. Career Growth : There are real opportunities to grow your career â I started as a Senior Machine Learning Engineer, then moved into a Senior Data Sience role, and am now a Staff AI Scientist. That progression shows how much the company invests in development and supports advancement when youâre willing to learn and take on new challenges. 2. Work-Life Balance: Work-life balance is truly respected here. Beyond flexible working, Intuit even gives us recharge days at the end of each year â during the holiday season (Christmas through December 31) â which really allows us to rest, reset ,enjoy our times with our families, and start the new year energized. 3. People & Culture: I get to work with amazing and wonderful colleagues. The culture is diverse, supportive, and collaborative â being surrounded by such talented and kind people makes a huge difference in day-to-day life.
cons: 1. The fast-paced environment may feel overwhelming at times, but it also provides constant opportunities to learn, grow, and make an impact quickly. 2. Organizational changes can sometimes be challenging to navigate, yet they often lead to new opportunities, innovation, and alignment with evolving business goals.

title: Great company with strong benefits and supportive culture
body: Great company with strong benefits and supportive culture
pros: Excellent benefits: Intuit contributes $1.25 for every $1 you contribute (up to 6% of eligible annual pay, max $10,000 per year) to your retirement plan. The ESPP program is also a great perk. Comprehensive health coverage and the Wellbeing for Life program, which reimburses up to $1,300 annually for physical, mental, and financial wellness expenses for you and your family. Clear career paths and strong manager support that help employees grow in their roles. I meet a lot of nice people here. They're really helpful and supportive. Overall culture is good. Collaborative culture where people are encouraged to bring ideas and innovate.
cons: The company is moving faster than before, so work pressure can be felt at times. Priorities sometimes shift due to upper leadership changes, which can impact focus. Reorganizations happen frequently (almost every year, sometimes twice a year). This can create uncertainty, though it also brings new opportunities depending on your perspective.

title: A Great Place to Grow
pros: Intuit truly values its employees. From day one, I felt supported and encouraged to grow both personally and professionally. Also, Intuit invests heavily in its employees. Whether itâs through internal training, mentorship programs, or opportunities to take on new challenges, Iâve always felt like my growth was a priority here.
cons: In certain roles, the workload can spike drastically during busy financial times (e.g., tax season). This requires strong time management and occasional long hours.

title: Best job ever
pros: What I enjoy most about my role at Intuit Finance is the opportunity to continuously learn and grow each day, enabling me to enhance my performance and excel in my responsibilities. Intuit places a strong emphasis on work-life balance, which is reflected in the flexible hybrid work model of three days on campus and two days working from home. Additionally, Intuit offers reimbursement for internet expenses incurred while working remotely, further demonstrating their commitment to supporting employees both in and out of the office. Another standout feature is the affordability of the campus food courtâbreakfast is typically just $3-$4, while lunch ranges from $5-$7. With prices like these, thereâs hardly a need for fast food! Intuit truly goes above and beyond to take care of its employees.

body: The culture I joined for is quickly disappearing
pros: Nice campus. Smart capable engineers. Mature technical stack.
cons: This place used to be a highly sought after employer. Employees were proud to work here, encouraged to architect sound code, and collaborate on proper solutions to achieve success. Now there is so much pressure on velocity that whoever can hack code the fastest gets rewarded. New features and offers are poorly vetted, rushed through to delivery, and produce disappointing outcomes. The stack ranking policy that has been implemented recently will surely finish off the good culture and positive morale here.

title: Worse termination policy than Amazon
pros: Good teams will treat you well, but things have taken a turn for the worse
cons: They mandated a in-house programming language that you will need to learn. Documentation for it is lacking and nobody is excited about it. Learning this language will not build your skillset as an engineer. They implemented a stack ranking policy. No pip, no support, no severence. Amazon at least gives you a runway before firing with severance so you can prepare finances, take leave, or apply elsewhere. Intuit will fire you for performance without warning, pip, OR severence. This will supposedly happen to 8% of people a year. This new performance termination policy is worse than Amazon.

title: How the mighty have fallen
pros: Great benefits, peers are great. I was able to stay fully remote since 2021
cons: CEO is running the company into the ground. There is a revolving door of sales and senior leadership. Everytime a new executive is hired they come in with an âaggressive â new sales target. This is fine but when there has been 6 new executives in 3 years and each one wants to 2x every metric we end up where we are at today. Layoffs accusing people of being low performers , only after they created a stacked rank system so that there will always be bottom performers by definition. No mobility into leadership. Mid market is a new initiative that hired 98% external account managers and leaders. The company culture is shot and I genuinely donât think intuit is headed in the right direction. Considering selling all of my stocks and getting all of my money out of credit karmas save accountâ¦

title: Truly Love Working Here!
cons: The only downside is the length of the season. Everyone starts as a seasonal employee, working an average of 4 months per year, so if you need something full-time permanent, this wouldn't be a great position for you. But, if you do a great job, you are almost guaranteed to return each tax season and there are promotion opportunities when you gain more experience. I actually enjoyed being seasonal when I started because I was free to spend time with my children during their summer breaks so the Tax Expert position is great for stay-at-home parents, retirees, and college students.

title: Intuit - The Future of Tax Preparation
body: Intuit - The Future of Tax Preparation
pros: Working as a Seasonal Tax Expert (my Second Season) at Intuit (TTLive) has been a great experience, I continuously seek opportunities to augments my professional skill set as a CPA, and Intuits provides the necessary platform, excellent AI integrated tax preparation software. If you are looking for a place to experience the future of Tax Preparation work. I highly recommend Intuit. My advise to future tax professionals, at Intuit be prepared to be fully engaged, assume, enjoy and take ownership of the process and workflow with respect to assigned clients.
cons: Intuit is ahead of the Tax Preparation Industry in the deployment of emerging technology. From my perspective this leans mostly in the positive.

title: Great Place to Work!

title: TurboTax for the season
pros: I'm retired. I can work from home, so this is a great seasonal job for the winter. I have flexible hours and a great relationship with my Manager. There isn't the typical pressure of the tax season.
cons: The pay is okay. Don't expect the rate of a private firm, but you can still have a life in the tax season.

title: Almost no pay increases, no benefits
body: Almost no pay increases, no benefits
pros: - Allows you to work from home and set your own schedule
cons: - Bonus has not been increased in years / does not even account for inflation - No benefits for seasonal employees other than sick pay and Internet reimbursement - Greatly reduced hours and incentives this year with no advanced noticed - Contracted to provide a minimum of 32 hours, which they did not fulfill - Did not advise employees hours were going to be greatly reduced this season disallowing employees the ability to make decisions best for their situation, greatly reduced the income due to cutting hours/overtime employees were expecting based on prior years with no noticed affecting financial outcomes poorly - Company culture completely changed this year with a coaching down approach expecting employees to provide feedback on how we can improve rather than a coaching up approach as used in years past, where we were provided with concrete, specific examples of how we can be better and improve professionally - Excessive pressure but on employees to be perfect, which literally is impossible

title: A most welcomed seasonal change!
pros: I look forward to feeling like I am apart of a grand mission. I truly appreciate the inspiration, the accomodations, and the rewards for just doing my job. The training provided, the communication channels, and the up to date laptop/camera/headphone tools really help one feel like the are set up and supported for a successfull mission.
cons: Like all seasons, it too comes to pass. There this dreadfull waiting period until they called you back and offered you a contract for the next season. I almost took another job because I afraid that I might not be called back. This is a normal fear for seasonal workers. But I am now spoiled by the treatment I received with Intuit, other employers did not make me feel as valued, apprecitated, and compensated for my time and continuous training efforts. At Intuit you can create such a work life balance with their flexible scheduling, empathetic first line supvervision, bonuses and branded "thank you for your service" gifts, it is hard to settle for anything less!

title: Excellent seasonal job!
pros: The best tax prep company to work for, hands down! Great pay, flexible schedule and great people! Incentives are offered along the way during the season to keep you going. I used to work at accounting firms that were full of unhappy people working themselves to death. I thought I hated tax until I started working for Intuit's Turbo Tax. The pay was better, my manager was kind to me and I enjoyed the customer interaction.
cons: Although everyone is nice, some of my co-workers are not the best educated/prepared for tax preparation.

title: A League of Their Own
pros: A company that is willing to put every effort to ensure the success of every individual. Flexible Work From Home Family Orientated Success Driven Excellent Work Life Balance
cons: I have absolutely nothing bad to say about Intuit. I love this company! NO CONS!!

title: Yes
pros: I have worked for turbo tax more than 6 years. I love the seasonal expert culture environment. As a CPA I have grown working for turbo tax. title: Balancing Opportunities and Challenges
pros: Working at Intuit's Tax division has offered quite a few advantages. The flexible scheduling allows for a good work-life balance, enabling employees to manage their time effectively. The team structure supports some level of autonomy, giving staff the freedom to innovate and take ownership of their work. Furthermore, the majority of colleagues are forward-thinking and maintain a positive attitude, which fosters a productive and encouraging work environment.
cons: Despite many positive aspects, there is a noticeable challenge stemming from a few longstanding employees who contribute to a negative atmosphere. This small group of individuals sometimes hinder the collaborative and innovative culture that Intuit strives to uphold.

title: Great Place to work
pros: Pros: -Leverage Your Credentials: You'll be able to fully utilize your Enrolled Agent (EA), CPA, or JD credential, applying your in-depth tax knowledge to assist customers with more complex tax situations. This can lead to more challenging and engaging work. -Higher Earning Potential: Credentialed roles often come with a higher hourly pay rate and potential for end-of-season bonuses based on performance. You may also receive a pay increase upon obtaining your credentials while working at TurboTax. -Career Advancement Opportunities: Holding a credential can open doors to leadership roles within TurboTax Live, such as a Tax Expert Lead or Manager position. Intuit actively encourages professional development and internal promotions. -Remote Work Flexibility: Many TurboTax Live positions, including lead roles, offer the flexibility of working remotely with the ability to set your own schedule to balance personal and professional life.

title: Excellent pay and benefits, but a bit chaotic and unorganized at times...
body: Excellent pay and benefits, but a bit chaotic and unorganized at times...
pros: Frist off, Intuit has been a GREAT place to work and was everything I hoped it would be! I've been thoroughly impressed with the pay and benefits at Intuit. In addition to highly competitive base pay and bonuses, you also have access to additional financial incentives like the 401k match, RSU stock grants, discounted stock purchase plans, and a variety of other "free money" opportunities. The office perks and amenities are above average as well. Free snacks, espresso machine, sparkling water machine, plus an on-site cafe offering the cheapest meals you can buy. We're also invited to participate in fun weekly activities that often results in some sort of small prize, swag, or dessert. The culture is inclusive and the people are kind and supportive. For the most part, everyone is collaborative and rallies around each other to get things done. Aside from some employees who were grandfathered in to full remote roles, most employees are hybrid which was just bumped up to three days a week in the office.
cons: Intuit has a lot of seemingly duplicative applications across the organization with no desire for standardization. It's often left up to the individual teams to determine how they want to work and the tools they use, which may not align with other teams. Priorities seem to change a lot. What's important this week may no longer be important next week. I understand the ever changing landscape of this business, but it's hard on employees who end up scrapping hard work to move on to something else. The hybrid work approach is great for flexibility, but can take a toll on employees who appreciate personal connection and a sense of belonging. This is especially true for virtual teams who are not physically co-located.

cons: I have no cons that I can think of regarding this job. I love it here!

title: Request for support in attracting tax and accounting experts to Intuit

pros: Easy to do Already referred individuals to Intuit Academy Great company to work for

title: Seasonal Preparations
pros: Flexibility is definitely an option here as long as if you are part time/seasonal/full time employee. I will speak more on my role as being part time/seasonal. - 20 hours a week - flexible - need to maintain a great schedule adherence above 90% - Have a great team to support you! Including leads from time to time. - all resources are supported internally through TT system. - You get bonuses and awards for being productive which is very motivating. - Resources are provided for free when wanting to level up or have professional development.
cons: - Some that were mentioned above can be a con - Having flexibility isn't always the best when you need to meet certain requirements as far as meeting specific hours that may be needed on the weekends, - You have to meet certain requirements before getting end of season bonus. - Atmosphere can get hectic. If you can't take the heat, stay out of the kitchen. - Survey rates can be negative for you as an Expert if you don't resolve an issue for the customer but it is not bad because most of the time it will not be you. The customer may have been dissatisfied with the system or because they are not getting a refund. - Need to stay productive at all times, breaks are limited, you get maybe a 15 min break when you work 5 hours, you get two 15's and a 30 minute lunch when you work over 7 hours.

title: Employee Satisfaction Driven
pros: Intuit actually appreciate your hard work and they show you in a lot of ways. My first season I treated as a side job but after 5 years its the job that I want to 100% into, It is very rewarding helping the customers and the delight in their voice or face when you do. They have great benefits for seasonal employees so I can only hope to become a Full time employee one day soon
cons: You have to able to pivot and be comfortable with change.

title: Great place to work
pros: Ease of scheduling (the majority of the time) There are times an email goes out that the schedule is available for change, then I can't add hours. My manager is great!
cons: It gets frustrating when the system does not work properly. Examples are cannot sign in, cannot connect to customer by video or by phone. Another example is having to input the same information six times over two days and the info does stay and I have to keep inputting it, losing time in the process. Cases have disappeared at random.

title: I receive calls from Customers that need assistance with preparing their return as well as assisting (DIY).

pros: I enjoy the Full Service. The experience is just like having a customer walk through your door looking to have their return completed, but just virtually. It provides the same service but just not physically in person.
cons: When taking Full Service calls, you are not receiving many chat or random call in questions.

Most of all, at Intuit, there is Work, Life, Balance. It's flexible, you pick your own schedule! how awesome is that!!

Peer to Peer learning opportunities for growth Meeting Moments to Team Bond and Revisit Learning Tools for changes. Constant Opportunities to meet deadlines and earn extra income during Peak Times. Fast growing and competitive with a sense of proud to be a part of the Intuit Growing Team

At other companies, I was expected to gatekeep information, that in my opinion, everyone should be privy to. I love the CAN DO atmosphere, not only encouraging our customers but being encouraged by our leads and managers. It is the least toxic company I have ever worked for and am very happy. I honestly love it. Flexible hours, great bonuses, great pay and perks, and a HEALTHY atmosphere!
cons: The only "con" might be having certain required hours to work throughout the busy weeks, but if you have any tax experience, you expect to be needed during both "peaks".

title: Intuit , a great place to work!
body: Intuit , a great place to work!
pros: Intuit is an awesome employer that offers a continuous growth process for it's employees. The company gives each employee an opportunity to reach their full potential through continuing education platforms. Working at Intuit has a great life /work balance that offers a hybrid work environment. They are supportive to their employees with a commitment to excellence by recognizing the importance of diversity, ideas, and consistent motivation by recognizing your internal achievements.
cons: Trying to keep up and strive for excellence in a fast pace environment.

title: Love this work!
pros: I help people! That is the best part. I am helping people that may be confused about their taxes. I love making sure that when I have completed an engagement the customer is happy to work with me and with TurboTax. And TurboTax/Intuit supports that effort. The provide me with the tools and the time to make my customers happy. I am certainly encouraged to be efficient. But not at the detriment of a happy customer. I work from home and the customer works from home (or wherever) to get the job done. I am happy not to commute. But also the customer can be at home where they keep their records. If I need something they can just look where they are quickly and easily. They don't have to go home, get the documents, and then come in hoping that is what I am looking for.
cons: I cannot think of any real cons.
role: Current employee - Remote Seasonal Tax Expert

title: Best Job in the World!
pros: This job allows me to be a fulltime mother at home. I am able to have a successful career and to have the honor to stay home with my children. Everyone is so encouraging and such a positive environment to work in. The hours are flexible so if I need to schedule around an event for my personal schedule I am able to do so. Lets not forget about the 401k match and bonus at the end of the season! Intuit really cares about who I am, my needs, and provides the tools to my success within the company! We are truly stronger together here and I am beyond grateful to be apart of such an amazing community and company!
cons: The only con that I have is when I am seasonal and I have to offboard and not see everyone for a few months. But luckily, there are full time positions offered to apply for!

Is it perfect no and I will explain that in the cons however, I am proud to work for this company, My manager is the best manager ever. title: Really Interesting Work
pros: I see a lot more variety of tax client than I did when I owned my own tax office. In many ways it is more interesting, I am not responsible for sighing other peoples tax returns. I give them legal and proper guidance. Sometimes I do tax returns but TT makes sure everything is documented. Most people are surprisingly pleasant. If you want to climb the corporate ladder there are plenty of opportunities. If you just want to keep your hand in after retirement from accounting you can do that too.
cons: Work from home is still work. You have to show up on time and do a proper job. Compared to face to face tax prep it is very pleasant.

title: Working at Intuit
pros: Working at Intuit has been a truly rewarding experience. One of the things I love most is the flexibility the the company offers. Whether it's remote work options, flexible hours, or support for work-life balance, Intuit truly understands that employees have lives outside of work-and that understanding makes a huge difference. The company also ha a strong family-oriented culture. From leadership to teammates, there's a genuine sense of care and community. Intuit supports not only your personal well-being but also your professional growth. I've felt encouraged and empowered to bring my whole self to work, which creates a positive and motivating environment every day. Overall, I'm grateful to be part of a company that values both people and purpose.
cons: Like any large company, processes can sometimes be slow, and navigating cross-functional teams takes patience. However, leadership is open to feedback and always looking to improve.

pros: *My Teammates - my teammates are awesome, respectful, helpful and driven by being Stronger Together which is continually encouraged by our manager. *Intuit being a company that drives its employees to be the Best they can be. *Meeting and delivering awesome service to our customers. Every customer matters - regardless of the simplicity or complexity of the tax issue. * I enjoy the spotlights - these make me smile and want to strive to do better. *My manager who embodies and preaches INTUIT's values during our huddles and 1:1. She makes me want to come to work every day. * I respect the Leads who go the distance with us experts, *Work Life Balance *Daily Huddle - this is so very beneficial to us all - TA, TE, Select staff, etc. * Call Drivers - great to talk about these in the Huddle. We learn a lot from each other.
cons: IEP hiccups - these can be very discouraging. I dislike when IEP shuts down when I am in the middle of a call or a return. IEP seems to be getting worse every year. Leads who come in to assist an expert, send a link that the expert has already looked at before reaching out, then are ready to leave believing they have helped.

This payment structure not only rewards hard work and dedication but also motivates employees to strive for excellence. Training Opportunities: Intuit invests in employee development through comprehensive training programs. These programs help employees enhance their skills, stay up-to-date with industry trends, and advance their careers within the company. title: You should Really Come Join Our Team
body: You should Really Come Join Our Team
pros: The pros about our company is first our awesome staff members. We Always work together as a team. If you like helping people and enjoy learning like myself, Intuit would be the best place to work for. The awards and recognitions are an awesome additive to this learning/work environment. Every good deed is never unnoticed with our company. At our company your skills of excellence and improvements are never unnoticed; you are always made to feel as you Matter here at intuit. The finally pros to this company is they supply you with all the materials to be successful with intuit, the ability to work from home, and you are able to customized your schedule at anytime to meet your individual. needs.
cons: The cons for me with my job is it's seasonal not year round. During the time off I am still at my desk waiting to work again. During that time off I miss helping customers and I miss working with my Team members during our break. During the tax season I'm not to pleased with having to cram in all the latest products and the updated tax information for that year. To be honest it is difficult to remember all that crammed in information. The time frame of learning is too short when the season begins.

title: "Fantastic Culture and Amazing People!
pros: upportive and Collaborative: Intuit is known for its friendly and collaborative work environment. Professional Growth: The company invests in its employees, providing numerous opportunities for development and advancement. Welcome and Inclusive: Employees feel welcome and valued when they join the company. Responsibility: People are given a lot of responsibility.
cons: Uneven Workloads and Excessive Hours: Some teams may experience a more relaxed pace, while others face consistently demanding workloads, potentially leading to long hours (over 12 hours a day) to meet targets. Unrealistic Goals and Micromanagement: Intuit's focus on rapid growth can lead to the setting of unrealistic targets, and micromanagement to ensure employees are focused on those goals, which can be stressful. Lack of Work-Life Balance: The pressure to meet targets and the emphasis on seizing opportunities can result in a poor work-life balance, with long hours becoming the norm.

pros: Nearly everything is a Pro working at Intuit. They VALUE diversity in pretty much every way. I am a disabled Employee with colored hair. They love it. I can have any color hair I want. The work culture is pretty amazing. We are allowed to hang up (after a warning) when a client/customer is yelling at us or being unkind. Full control of your own schedule. Advancing into management type positions is much easier than most places.
cons: Must cover tattoos (a con for those it may matter to) and keeping up with tech/tool updates can be frustrating... The updates happen pretty regularly. Becoming a Full Year employee is not easy as tons internally apply each year and usually there are very few openings for Full Year positions.

The teams mostly consists of people impersonating "Software Engineers" who have mastered their political skills. Make sure that you are good with praising everyone. All teams consists of someone who is a myth, a legend and are used to hearing that a lot even during lunch. Thats the only way to survive, when I quit after only 4 months my manager asked if I was laid of from the previous company and tried finding some reason other than what I mentioned as being no work. With the introduction of pip culture, the middle managers are hiring to fire so that they can protect their loved ones. Please run away.

Free tax filing for employees.
cons: The position is seasonal but there are full-time positions available if you apply and are selected. But usually the work lasts for 5 months and if selected to extend for summer season, can last 9. All year employment is what I would prefer.

title: Review for Intuit TurboTaxLive
pros: I have had an excellent experience working at Intuit TurboTax. The work environment is incredibly supportive, collaborative, and friendly. One of the highlights of my time here has been the amazing opportunities for professional growth. Additionally, I want to commend my manager ( Victoria Adams) and the HR team for their exceptional support. When I was ill and needed medical help, they worked closely and actively to ensure I received the assistance I needed. Their care and dedication made a significant difference during a challenging time.
cons: None that I can think of at the moment

cons: They are so tech based that I think sometimes that gets in the way. But the growth and continued effort for improvement far outweighs the challenges of navigating new tech ideas and paths

There is a healthy push from the leadership towards experimenting with the newest frameworks which will help you as an engineer to keep up to date with the latest technologies. The workload is pretty balanced and the benefits are also great and at par with other FAANG companies.
cons: Typically sofware development triad (Dev, Product Managers, Experience Design) works best when all three pillars are equally empowered. But currently, there is a slight engineering bias in the teams. There is an opportunity for the product and especially the design teams to be strengthened.

title: Amazing Promotion Process
pros: The evaluation process is designed remarkably, involving input from both managers and skip-level managers. The final assessment is conducted by a Bar-raiser, who is external to your current organization, ensuring neutrality in most instances. Throughout each stage of the process, from profile building to subsequent phases, extensive feedback is provided. The overall process is highly transparent, fostering a clear understanding of expectations and outcomes.
cons: The overall process can be somewhat lengthy as it encompasses multiple phases, including several interviews, document preparation, and profile building.

title: A sinking ship
pros: - Good perks such as 401k match, $1300 wellness budget, discounted rentals - 2 days a week RTO (not strictly enforced though)
cons: - The worst Data Science you could ever imagine. The team is puppet of other business stakeholders (such as Product, Sales, etc.) and the priorities change almost every other week - Data leaders lack spine. They let other stakeholders treat DS as a support team rather than thought leaders or drivers of KPIs - While other companies let their DS spend 30-50% time in deep dives or strategic analyses, it would be a good quarter for you if you could spend even 20% of your bandwidth on analyses. Most of the time goes into ad-hoc, dashboarding, biz reporting, etc. - Poor data infrastructure - Too much politics and favoritism for promotions and recognitions

title: Would be excellent work if only the training prepared you better for actual role
body: Would be excellent work if only the training prepared you better for actual role
pros: They care about your well-being. They have a good team atmosphere. There is a lot of diversity so you do get understanding in that way. Management does listen, and tries to find solutions.
cons: The training for the role I was in was not adequate for the variety of types of calls that can come to you. They range from answering a quick question about where to put a number in the software to preparing a full tax return. You can have a text chat or a verbal call. No role play is done in the training. All the training is basically videos. It is not adequate.

title: Amazing company, supportive leadership
pros: - The culture is amazing. I feel taken care of and respected - Benefits are good, I love the Wellness for life benefit and all the resources Intuit provides- virtually and at the offices. - The work is challenging and exciting! - Leadership (from my experience) truly cares about your growth, if you are dedicated to growing, they are dedicated to guiding you - GED! Time set for engineers to build anything they want! (Within the scope of making business impact)
cons: - Compared to other tech companies, it seems Intuit is a bit on the lower end of the pay grade - Highly interconnected teams and architectures can create lots of friction if trying to make your own POCs and big independent initiatives - Often I find that backend documentation is non-existent or extremely difficult to find - Sometimes it feels there is not enough resources (engineers) on teams, often setting projects back and causing blockers

title: Amazing place to work
pros: Intuit is an incredible company to work for. There's flexibility with the hybrid work model. Managers are supportive and the company really fosters an inclusive environment. Amazing benefits and high performers are rewarded/compensated.
cons: Mobility can be limited for some teams other than going into management positions.

title: Company toxic management and culture
